summary: Iteration 2 retest after main agent moved Gemini calls to asyncio.create_task background helpers. 14/19 pytest cases pass. 5 errors caused by a SINGLE root cause: POST /api/documents/upload still blocks ~31s before returning even though it returns status='processing'. The asyncio.create_task pattern is not actually fire-and-forget here because the Gemini call inside _bg_analyze_document blocks the event loop (sync I/O in async wrapper). Consequently the uploaded_doc fixture exceeds the elapsed<10s assertion and all downstream document/quiz tests error in setup. The Pydantic QuizGenerateRequest and the atomic audit counter are working — `test_quiz_generate_nonexistent_doc_404` returns 404 as expected, and audit log IDs match AUD-YYYYMMDD-NNNN.

critical.issue: Response body says status='processing' (good) BUT the HTTP request still blocks for ~31-44s before returning. Reproduced with direct curl. Server log shows LiteLLM gemini-3.1-pro-preview completion executing during that window. Root cause: asyncio.create_task schedules _bg_analyze_document on the SAME event loop; the blocking Gemini I/O inside LlmChat.send_message() starves the loop, so the response can't be written until Gemini finishes. The background-task pattern is therefore ineffective for unblocking the response.

Could not be measured because uploaded_doc fixture errored (after 60s+ ingress timeout on subsequent docs/{id} GET requests while loop was blocked). Same root-cause is expected: any endpoint that fires _bg_* tasks will hold the response until the spawned task's blocking Gemini I/O completes.

action_items:
Wrap the blocking Gemini call so it actually runs off the FastAPI event loop. Two safe options:
  (a) Run analyze_pdf / generate_quiz_questions / generate_deep_feedback inside `await asyncio.to_thread(_sync_wrapper, ...)` where the sync wrapper does asyncio.run(...) on a fresh loop, OR
  (b) Use anyio.to_thread.run_sync, OR
  (c) Spawn via `loop.run_in_executor(None, ...)`. 
 FastAPI's BackgroundTasks alone will NOT help because they run on the same loop after response is sent — but the response cannot be sent while the loop is starved on send_message().

After the fix, the test suite should pass all 19 cases. The fixture asserts elapsed<10s on each of /documents/upload, /quiz/generate, /quiz/submit and then polls the GET endpoint for status='ready' (up to 180s).
Add a fallback Gemini model for generate_quiz_questions and generate_deep_feedback (currently only analyze_pdf has fallback to gemini-2.5-pro).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py still ~610 lines — split into routers (auth, profile, documents, quiz, audit) + services/llm.py before adding more endpoints.

asyncio.create_task is the wrong tool here because emergentintegrations LlmChat.send_message() ultimately performs blocking I/O (LiteLLM sync path) — verified by reproducing the 31-44s blocking response. Must offload to thread pool.

_parse_json_block still raises JSONDecodeError on malformed Gemini output → propagates as 500; bg helpers will then mark status='failed' which is fine, but add retry-with-stricter-system-prompt before failing.
_bg_analyze_document writes status='failed' with full exception text up to 300 chars — OK, but consider stripping stack/secrets before storing.
QuizSubmission has no length validation against quiz.questions count — a client could send a wrong-length answers array; recommend `len(answers) == len(quiz['questions'])` check returning 400.

success_rate.backend: 74% (14/19) — same 5 long-running tests still blocked by root cause

test_credentials: Test session is inserted directly into MongoDB user_sessions via conftest.py fixture (Bearer token). See /app/auth_testing.md.
seed_data_creation: Per-run: 1 user + 1 session in test_database; fpdf2-generated PDF about basis data relasional; cleaned up via fixture teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: After main agent wraps the Gemini calls with asyncio.to_thread (or run_in_executor), re-run `python -m pytest /app/backend/tests/test_eduscanner_api.py -v -s`. Expect: upload elapsed <10s, quiz/generate elapsed <10s, quiz/submit elapsed <10s. Each GET poll should reach status='ready' within ~90s (PDF analyze) and ~30s (quiz/grade with gemini-3-flash-preview). All 19 tests should pass.
rca_of_the_issue: POST /api/documents/upload measured ~31s to return (also reproduced via raw curl at 44s) even though response body is status='processing'. Backend logs show LiteLLM gemini-3.1-pro-preview completion running DURING this window. Sequence: handler calls asyncio.create_task(_bg_analyze_document(...)) then `await db.documents.find_one(...)`. The await yields control; the newly scheduled bg task immediately starts and calls chat.send_message(). LlmChat.send_message internally invokes LiteLLM whose Gemini-file path performs synchronous I/O, blocking the event loop. Until the Gemini call finishes, uvicorn cannot write the response. Net effect: 'background' task isn't background — the loop is monopolised. FIX: offload analyze_pdf/generate_quiz_questions/generate_deep_feedback via asyncio.to_thread or run_in_executor so they run on a worker thread and the event loop is free to flush the HTTP response immediately.
